_schema_info.name: Murphy TA Assistant — Knowledge Schema Template
_schema_info.purpose: Master blueprint that every module JSON file must follow. Ensures the AI reasoning engine can read all modules consistently.
_schema_info.author: Murphy TA Assistant Project
_schema_info.instructions: Copy this file, rename it to your module (e.g. trend.json), remove all _comment fields, and fill in your data.

module._comment: Unique identifier for this knowledge module. Use snake_case. Must match the filename.
module.name: Module Display Name
module.description: What this module covers in 1-2 sentences.

concepts._comment: All core concepts from Murphy for this module. Each concept = one TA idea with a clear definition.

_item_schema.name: Concept Display Name
_item_schema.definition: Precise definition of this concept as Murphy describes it.
_item_schema.murphy_context: How Murphy specifically uses or explains this concept in the book.

_example.name: Primary Trend
_example.definition: The longest-term price movement, typically lasting one year or more. Also called the major trend.
_example.murphy_context: Murphy treats the primary trend as the dominant force all shorter-term analysis must be evaluated against.

reasoning_rules._comment: Actionable if-then rules the AI uses to analyze charts. Each rule has a condition and a signal it produces.

_item_schema.rule: Plain English statement of the rule.
_item_schema.condition: The specific chart condition that must be present for this rule to apply.
_item_schema.murphy_basis: Brief note on which Murphy principle this rule is grounded in.

_example.rule: An uptrend is intact as long as each rally reaches a higher high and each pullback holds a higher low.
_example.condition: Price makes a new high above the previous rally high AND the last pullback low is above the prior pullback low.
_example.murphy_basis: Murphy's core definition of an uptrend from Chapter 4.

confirmations._comment: What strengthens or confirms a signal. Used by the AI to tell you how much confidence to place in a setup.

_item_schema.pattern: The primary pattern or signal being assessed.

confirmed_by.description: How this factor confirms the primary pattern.

contradicted_by.description: How this factor weakens or contradicts the primary pattern.

confirmed_by.description: Volume increases as price moves higher, confirming buyer participation.
Volume decreases during pullbacks, confirming selling pressure is weak.

contradicted_by.description: High volume on down days signals increasing selling pressure, weakening the trend.

invalidations._comment: The exact conditions that break or invalidate a concept or setup. This is how the AI knows when a thesis is wrong.

_item_schema.concept_id: the concept or setup being watched
_item_schema.invalidated_when: The specific price or volume action that breaks this concept.
_item_schema.what_to_monitor: What the analyst should watch to detect early signs of invalidation.
_item_schema.action_on_invalidation: What conclusion to draw — not a trade recommendation, a reasoning conclusion.
_item_schema.murphy_basis: Which Murphy principle defines this invalidation.

_example.invalidated_when: Price breaks below the most recent significant higher low on closing basis.
_example.what_to_monitor: The last swing low. A close below it is the first warning. A second lower low confirms trend change.
_example.action_on_invalidation: Uptrend structure is broken. Reassess bias. Do not assume trend resumption without new evidence.
_example.murphy_basis: Murphy's rule that an uptrend requires a series of higher highs AND higher lows. One lower low breaks the sequence.

knowledge_metadata._comment: Filled in after the module is complete. Used by the RAG retrieval layer to index and score this module.
knowledge_metadata.notes: Any notes on gaps, TODOs, or things to revisit.
